proofreader.note: All 17 chapters within 70-130% band; 12 bridge citations reworked & re-verified in iteration 2.

dropped_sections.reason: Memoir-style opening about Brooks's reserved upbringing and emotional thawing; vivid voice but no framework or data; condensed into a single bridge.
Anecdotes (Oprah, bat at baseball game) restating the same point about Brooks's growth; second illustrations, score ≤3.
Elaboration on creeping dehumanization and social media; descriptive padding after the recognition point lands.
Three-reasons scaffolding (pragmatic/spiritual/survival) condensed; the McKinsey data point preserved in bridge.
Forster, Gladstone/Disraeli anecdotes—additional illustrations of the Illuminator concept already shown via Nyquist.
Empathic-accuracy elaboration and rhetorical questions; repeats the point made by the Ickes data anchor.
The orchid-doorway beholding scene; vivid but the key line is captured in the anchor; surrounding paragraphs are descriptive.
Bar-scene size-up vignette and Bobos reference; voice padding before the Diminisher list.
Anxiety, Naïve Realism, Objectivism, Static Mindset entries; representative items kept (egotism, lesser-minds, essentialism), the rest dropped to avoid list bloat.
Extended Gornick memoir narrative; condensed into a bridge with the analytic payoff kept as anchor.
Waco/Jimmy Dorrell setup; narrative captured in bridge, the attention principle kept as anchor.
Soul/reverence passage and the six gaze-feature entries with Tolstoy quote; representative anchors kept, illustrative entries condensed.
Murdoch's M-and-D mother-in-law illustration; second example of an idea already established by the just-and-loving-attention anchor.
Mary Pipher profile; condensed into a bridge as an extended single illustration.
Loren Eiseley river-float narrative; descriptive frame condensed; principle stated in accompaniment anchors.
Patience/playfulness/other-centeredness sub-sections with multiple anecdotes (Caldwell, baseball coaching); condensed; presence kept as anchor.
Long Carrère tsunami narrative; condensed into a bridge, with the Huxley payoff line as anchor.
Yalom and Anaïs Nin illustrations of subjectivity; second examples after Brackett anchor.
Brain-science deep dive and perception experiments; key mechanism kept as anchor, experiments condensed to bridge.

Favor Familiarity, Don't Fear the Pause, Midwife, Gem Statement entries; representative conversational moves kept, others condensed to limit list length.

Niobe Way interview and questioner-percentage anecdotes; color before the moral-practice anchor.
Question-type taxonomy and big-question lists; condensed; humble-open-question rule kept as anchor.

Politics-of-recognition and mass-shooter sections; extended argument condensed to a bridge to control chapter share.

Culture-war panel narrative and Ellison Invisible Man passage; extended illustration condensed.
Splitting/repair moves and Proffitt hill-perception experiments; condensed to bridge; affordances kept as anchor.

Styron/Solomon depression quotes and Gerson sermon; supporting illustrations of the presence lesson already anchored.
Childhood-defenses section, Grant Study warmth findings, sacred-flaw and conceptual-blindness illustrations; condensed into bridges; empathy-skill anchors retained.
Emotion-construction mechanics, granularity examples, and empathy-spectrum/Carol case; representative anchors kept, the rest condensed.
Empathy-training practices (contact theory, acting, literature, mood meter) and co-regulation; condensed into a bridge with the floor anecdote anchored.

Frederick Buechner father-suicide narrative; long single story condensed to a bridge with the steel-quote anchor.
Excavation exercises and Kushner reflection; instructional list condensed; warrior-vs-Illuminator model anchored.
Warrior/statesman model description and Murdoch coda; supporting contrast for the anchored Illuminator-character point.
George W. Bush extroversion anecdotes (Bullock kiss); vivid first example condensed into a bridge.
Conscientiousness and agreeableness entries plus Nettle/Brontë illustrations; representative Big Five anchors kept (extroversion, neuroticism, openness), others condensed.
Self-assessment guidance and Danielle Dick parenting application; instructional padding after the trait anchors.
Baby-development opening (Gopnik) and developmental-psychology framing; condensed into a bridge.

Four closing case studies (Kidder/Deo, Gottlieb/John, Good Will Hunting, Schulz's father); long narratives condensed into one bridge with two thematic anchors retained.
